violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. No employee certified through an approved food safety management course was available at the beginning of the inspection. Owner/operator arrived during inspection and is certified through a food safety management course. Establishment does have an active FMC. More employees are to be certified through an approved food safety management course. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Raw shell eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from (below) raw ready-to-eat food during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Sanitizer bottle observed to be empty. New sanitizer bottle was hooked up, and dishwasher was run several times until the concentration of chlorine registered 100 ppm per test strip. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Thermometer is lacking in the walk-in cooler. All cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located inside the unit to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The door to the outside, located to the side of the 3-bay sink, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Utensils observed displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets of one of the prep units observed in poor repair, splitting away from the door. Gaskets are to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2026
Leak observed under the 3-bay sink. Plumbing must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

The door to the outside, located to the side of the 3-bay sink, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2025
A couple of refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair, splitting away from the doors. Gaskets are to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2025
The door to the restroom is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2025
